description: lower SCALAR_LR from 0.20 to 0.15 — continuing the downward trend that worked for depth-2
hypothesis: 0.25→0.20 helped for depth-2; 0.15 may help further given more gradient steps
next_move: if worse, 0.20 is the floor; if better, try 0.10

replacements.old: SCALAR_LR = 0.20        # learning rate for per-layer scalars (Adam)
replacements.new: SCALAR_LR = 0.15        # learning rate for per-layer scalars (Adam)

description: raise MATRIX_LR from 0.035 to 0.04 — continuing the upward trend for Muon in depth-2
hypothesis: 0.03→0.035 helped; 0.04 may help further given more gradient steps available
next_move: if worse, 0.035 is the ceiling; if better, try 0.045

replacements.old: MATRIX_LR = 0.035       # learning rate for matrix parameters (Muon)
replacements.new: MATRIX_LR = 0.040       # learning rate for matrix parameters (Muon)

description: raise WARMDOWN_RATIO from 0.35 to 0.40 — continuing the trend toward more cooldown in depth-2
hypothesis: 0.30→0.35 improved; the depth-2 model with more steps may benefit from even more gradual cooldown
next_move: if worse, 0.35 is optimal for depth-2

replacements.old: WARMDOWN_RATIO = 0.35   # fraction of time budget for LR warmdown
replacements.new: WARMDOWN_RATIO = 0.40   # fraction of time budget for LR warmdown

description: lower EMBEDDING_LR from 0.45 to 0.40 — was worse for depth-3 but depth-2 LRs are generally lower
hypothesis: SCALAR_LR and MATRIX_LR both shifted for depth-2; embedding LR may also want to move
next_move: if worse, 0.45 is correct; if better, try 0.35

replacements.old: EMBEDDING_LR = 0.45     # learning rate for token embeddings (Adam)
replacements.new: EMBEDDING_LR = 0.40     # learning rate for token embeddings (Adam)

description: raise UNEMBEDDING_LR from 0.005 to 0.006 — was badly worse for depth-3 but depth-2 may tolerate it
hypothesis: the lm_head may need a stronger signal in the depth-2 regime given fewer parameters total
next_move: if worse, 0.005 is still correct; if better, the pattern has shifted

description: raise WEIGHT_DECAY from 0.2 to 0.25 — more regularization with higher MATRIX_LR
hypothesis: MATRIX_LR increased from 0.03 to 0.035; slightly more weight decay may compensate and prevent Muon overshooting
next_move: if worse, weight decay was fine at 0.2; if better, the higher LR needs more regularization

replacements.old: WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.2      # cautious weight decay for Muon
replacements.new: WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.25     # cautious weight decay for Muon
